7.reason: [The `gets` function is unsafe and has been removed from the C standard library since C11; many modern compilers reject it, causing a compilation error.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.25 , reason:  [The `gets` function is unsafe and has been removed from the C standard library since C11; many modern compilers reject it, causing a compilation error.]

16.reason: [If the string starts with a number (no leading space), this loop may access s[j] with j < 0, causing undefined behavior. Also, logic for parsing numbers is flawed as it assumes digits are contiguous backward without checking bounds properly.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.25 , reason:  [If the string starts with a number (no leading space), this loop may access s[j] with j < 0, causing undefined behavior. Also, logic for parsing numbers is flawed as it assumes digits are contiguous backward without checking bounds properly.]

54.reason: [Same issue as above: potential out-of-bounds access when j goes below 0 if there's no space before the last number.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.25 , reason:  [Same issue as above: potential out-of-bounds access when j goes below 0 if there's no space before the last number.]

66.reason: [Condition `j>=k||(i==n2-1)` is logically incorrect; when i==n2-1, this branch shouldn't execute because the outer if already handles i==n2-1. This leads to double-processing or invalid memory access.]
